You can see that it takes an image and turns it into an animation or video. That's really cool because when it'll increase conversion rates, just how many people will see it and click on it. It will also help with platforms push video more because it keeps users on the platform longer. So this is just a sample. We'll do a new project, a new project. The longer the line, obviously the less subtle the movement. So we can press play here. I like that. And again, you'll have to play with what looks good. Some images don't look great like this. To tighten this up, which I recommend, you want to put these anchor points around. Usually the periphery, it just makes less things move. So it's more focused. This one actually turned out pretty well. I was playing with this one earlier and I think this would definitely convert. Other things you can do with it is you can add overlays. So just like cool effects here, this will definitely help draw attention. You know, based off of the mood, the time of the year, things like that. There's a lot of cool ones even for free in here. Things I would really recommend would be like fireworks, fire lightning, things like that. Honestly, that's most of it. If you don't like something, you can just press that undo, of course. I would tighten this up more if this was an ad I was running, but those are the main things. Again, you can play with camera effects and like this, there's a lot of great stuff. Top right is going to export it. You can increase duration. I would stick to 69 or 11 for ads. 1080p is fine. If you have a really good camera or new iPhone or something, you could go 4k. Then you'll just export from there. It's going to save it to a camera roll and you can take it into Drive or Dropbox if you need. But just play around with it.
